name: Raman-ChEMBL-part2
description: 140k DFT-computed Raman spectra for ChEMBL drug-like molecules. Targets: HOMO-LUMO gap, HOMO/LUMO energies, isotropic polarizability, heat capacity, dipole moment.
citeAs: @article{liang2025dataset, title={A Dataset of Raman and Infrared Spectra as an Extension to the ChEMBL}, author={Liang, Jiechun and Ling, Jack and Xu, Limin and Zhu, Xi}, journal={Scientific Data}, volume={12}, number={1}, pages={939}, year={2025}, publisher={Nature Publishing Group UK London}}
citation: Liang, J., Ling, J., Zhu, X. Raman-ChEMBL-part2. figshare. Dataset. https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.28594295.v3

distribution.name: Raman-ChEMBL-part2
distribution.description: Figshare article hosting all data files.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Target value(s): concentration, physical property, or other continuous measurement.

rai:dataCollection: DFT Raman spectra computed for ~220,000 drug-like molecules from the ChEMBL database using Gaussian09 (PBEPBE/6-31G level of theory) in gas phase. Harmonic frequencies and Raman activities stored as stick spectra; Lorentz broadening applied for continuous spectral representation. Molecular property targets computed at the same DFT level.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Raw DFT harmonic frequencies and Raman activities stored as stick spectra. Lorentz broadening applied to produce continuous spectral profiles. No further preprocessing is applied to the provided files.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Molecular properties (HOMO, LUMO, band gap Eg, isotropic polarizability, heat capacity, dipole moment) computed via DFT at PBEPBE/6-31G level using Gaussian09 in gas phase. Raman activities derived from harmonic frequency calculations; spectra broadened by Lorentz fitting. Approximately 220,000 ChEMBL drug-like molecules processed; ~140,000 included after filtering.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information. All spectra and properties are computationally derived from chemical structures in the public ChEMBL database.
rai:dataBiases: Spectra are DFT-computed (gas phase, PBEPBE/6-31G) and do not account for solvent effects, temperature broadening, or instrument-specific artefacts present in experimental Raman spectra. 96.3% of molecules are aromatic; non-aromatic drug-like molecules are underrepresented. Computational accuracy is limited by the DFT functional and basis set; high-frequency modes may be overestimated under the harmonic approximation.
rai:dataLimitations: Gas-phase DFT calculations do not capture solvent effects or conformational flexibility at finite temperature. The PBEPBE/6-31G level of theory provides moderate accuracy; harmonic approximation may overestimate high-frequency modes. Direct comparison with experimental spectra requires careful scaling and calibration. Licensed CC BY-NC-ND 4.0; commercial use is not permitted.
rai:dataSocialImpact: A large-scale DFT Raman spectral library for drug-like molecules enables virtual screening, spectral prediction, and training of deep learning models for molecular property prediction, supporting computational drug discovery and materials science research.
rai:dataUseCases: DFT spectral library for computational chemistry; training spectral prediction models; molecular property regression (HOMO, LUMO, band gap, polarizability, heat capacity, dipole moment); benchmarking machine learning potentials. Not recommended for commercial use (CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 license). Not recommended for direct comparison with experimental spectra without frequency scaling and instrument calibration.
